summary: Resolve the installed 7-Zip version from signed Windows file metadata so the exact-installer cleanroom preflight can proceed without weakening tool provenance.

decisions.decision: Read 7-Zip's ProductVersion from the resolved executable's Windows VersionInfo instead of invoking the unsupported --version argument.
decisions.rationale: The no-soak cleanroom wrapper failed closed before any build or Sandbox launch because 7-Zip 26.01 exits nonzero for --version. The installed executable exposes ProductVersion 26.01 through Windows file metadata, which preserves exact tool-version provenance without parsing usage text or accepting a failed command.

testEvidence.criterion: The cleanroom wrapper resolves a nonempty exact 7-Zip version without invoking an unsupported CLI argument.
testEvidence.test: Require the wrapper to use Get-FileProductVersion for 7-Zip and reject regression to Get-ToolVersion, then parse the PowerShell source and inspect the installed executable metadata.

red.command: powershell.exe -NoLogo -NoProfile -NonInteractive -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/windows/run-packaged-desktop-evidence.ps1 [exact final-main evidence arguments] -N2SoakMode disabled
red.outcome: RED: the wrapper stopped before build and Sandbox launch with 'Could not resolve 7-Zip version' because 7z.exe --version exits nonzero.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/packaged-desktop-evidence.test.ts -t "fails closed before staging unless a clean HEAD is built at the canonical target" --reporter=default; PowerShell parser check; read 7z.exe VersionInfo.ProductVersion
green.outcome: GREEN: the focused wrapper contract passed, PowerShell parsed without errors, and the resolved 7-Zip file product version was 26.01.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
